<!-- agent-py-sidecar: auto-generated; do not delete this line to allow refresh -->
# backend/routers/openclaw.py
# auto-generated explainer · 2026-06-05T10:00:46Z

## Purpose
OpenClaw bridge API.

HTTP layer for the repo-local OpenClaw compatibility bridge. It delegates all
queue and polling behavior to OpenClawGatewayService so tests can replace the
service without requiring Redis.

## Imports
- from `__future__` import annotations
- from `fastapi` import APIRouter, Depends, Request, Response
- from `core` import idempotency
- from `core.middleware` import current_tenant_id
- from `schemas.openclaw` import OpenClawManifestResponse, OpenClawMode, OpenClawStatusResponse, OpenClawTaskRequest, OpenClawTaskResponse
- from `services.openclaw_gateway_service` import OpenClawGatewayService

## Inputs
(no CLI / env inputs — library module or implicit)

## Process
### Functions
- `get_openclaw_service()`: Dependency provider for OpenClawGatewayService.
- `get_status(service)`: Return bridge availability and Redis queue status.
- `get_manifest(service)`: Return the OpenClaw bridge contract and governance notes.
- `create_task(http_request, response, request, service)`: Create an OpenClaw bridge task and enqueue it for workers.
- `get_task_result(task_id, mode, service)`: Poll a queued OpenClaw bridge task result.

## Outputs
- Return values from functions above

## Notable inline comments
- Idempotency check FIRST so retry storms don't re-enqueue the same task.
